title: The .precept file rendered as a syntax-colored code minimap. Each horizontal band is a line of code; the pattern of colors is the anatomy of a Precept definition: blue for declarations, teal for states, amber for transition rules, bright green for the governing operator. The absent final line is the structurally impossible path — too short to reach a destination.
conceptualBridge: The syntax bands mean Precept's contract is a complete structured file because the color pattern encodes the exact anatomy of a .precept definition — declarations, states, events, rules, and the absent impossible path — all visible at once.
decision: Kept: code minimap is a genuinely new visual family (4.06 avg), passes threshold; monochrome test is the weak point — consider a version with stronger structural differentiation that works without color.
